posts.name: Ry@n S m 1t h
posts.cooked: Is Kalin Cellars still in operation? I went on their website to see what thier current offerings are but looks like the website hasn’t been updated since April 2018. Has anyone heard any updates?

posts.name: Michael Baryla
posts.cooked:
I’ve emailed them a few times with no response. That being said my local go to Chinese food place (Uncle Yu’s in Livermore) seemed to get a shipment in not long ago. They are pouring their Chardonnay (94 I think) and Semillon (2001) by the glass! Kinda nuts in my opinion as this is definitely not everyones style. I get the “this isn’t your typical white wine” spiel every time I order it.

BTW - Totally off topic but Uncle Yu’s has a great wine list. Unfortunately their recent list update included price revisions. Not too bad but there used to be some STEALS on there. Great food too.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: Yes, still operational! Current releases are 2001 Sémillon, 1996 Livermore Valley Chardonnay, and 2000 Pinot Noir DD.

posts.name: Brian Buzzini
posts.cooked:
BTW - Totally off topic but Uncle Yu’s has a great wine list. Unfortunately their recent list update included price revisions. Not too bad but there used to be some STEALS on there. Great food too.

Cool list! Definitely a Kalin honey hole!

posts.name: Chris Seiber
posts.cooked:
Yes, still operational! Current releases are 2001 Sémillon, 1996 Livermore Valley Chardonnay, and 2000 Pinot Noir DD.

Thanks. Does “still operational” mean (1) they still have some old inventory to sell off but aren’t making wines anymore, or (2) they are still making wines and will eventually sell more recent releases in the decades to some as they sell through current release and the wines hit the desired age?

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
Yes, still operational! Current releases are 2001 Sémillon, 1996 Livermore Valley Chardonnay, and 2000 Pinot Noir DD.

Thanks. Does “still operational” mean (1) they still have some old inventory to sell off but aren’t making wines anymore, or (2) they are still making wines and will eventually sell more recent releases in the decades to some as they sell through current release and the wines hit the desired age?

That you would have to ask them about! But they definitely have a bunch more stuff waiting to release in due course.

posts.name: Lawrence Leichtman
posts.cooked:
Is Kalin Cellars still in operation? I went on their website to see what thier current offerings are but looks like the website hasn’t been updated since April 2018. Has anyone heard any updates?

Don’t know. Quit buying after we got 3 flawed bottles. Had been buying from them for more than 10 years before that.

They stunk. Never could put my finger on it but likely, Brett.

posts.name: Brandon Econ
posts.cooked:
Yes, still operational! Current releases are 2001 Sémillon, 1996 Livermore Valley Chardonnay, and 2000 Pinot Noir DD.

Thanks. Does “still operational” mean (1) they still have some old inventory to sell off but aren’t making wines anymore, or (2) they are still making wines and will eventually sell more recent releases in the decades to some as they sell through current release and the wines hit the desired age?

That you would have to ask them about! But they definitely have a bunch more stuff waiting to release in due course.

Which feels like a catch 22 because they don’t respond to emails or answer the phone. Oh well.

posts.name: John Butler
posts.cooked: Good to know! I was wondering this as well. I love their wines and grab them when I see them retail. I received an email from Terry in late 2019 for the 1996 Livermore chard and the 2000 DD pinot - for “Holiday Shipping Season.” I had not received an email from the mailing list before that and haven’t received one since, but hopefully one of these days I’ll get an offer for some new wines!

posts.name: Vince T s 3 n g
posts.cooked:
Good to know! I was wondering this as well. I love their wines and grab them when I see them retail. I received an email from Terry in late 2019 for the 1996 Livermore chard and the 2000 DD pinot - for “Holiday Shipping Season.” I had not received an email from the mailing list before that and haven’t received one since, but hopefully one of these days I’ll get an offer for some new wines!

FWIW, K&L has the wines William mentioned above in stock.

I’ve opened several Kalin 95’ Livermore in the past year and have a few left. Seems like half of them have been fantastic and half have tasted a little off. They are a fun one to open with wino buddies as most people can’t believe the way they release their wines. I always look forward to opening them as it’s always a fun gamble.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
Good to know! I was wondering this as well. I love their wines and grab them when I see them retail. I received an email from Terry in late 2019 for the 1996 Livermore chard and the 2000 DD pinot - for “Holiday Shipping Season.” I had not received an email from the mailing list before that and haven’t received one since, but hopefully one of these days I’ll get an offer for some new wines!

FWIW, K&L has the wines William mentioned above in stock.

2001 Sémillon is especially good.

posts.name: James Sanders
posts.cooked:
I’ve emailed them a few times with no response. That being said my local go to Chinese food place (Uncle Yu’s in Livermore) seemed to get a shipment in not long ago. They are pouring their Chardonnay (94 I think) and Semillon (2001) by the glass! Kinda nuts in my opinion as this is definitely not everyones style. I get the “this isn’t your typical white wine” spiel every time I order it.

BTW - Totally off topic but Uncle Yu’s has a great wine list. Unfortunately their recent list update included price revisions. Not too bad but there used to be some STEALS on there. Great food too.

Do you know whether Uncle Yu’s in Livermore is linked in any way with the old Yujean’s in the East Bay? They also had an insane wine list. I remember having the 1980 Kalin Semillon and a Williams Selyem Pinot off their list one night at very reasonable prices. That 1980 Semillon is still my most memorable California white wine. Like a chameleon in overdrive. Every sip was a little different.
